meta.name: KG-2
meta.description: Same-model (gpt-5.2 x gpt-5.2) echo chamber experiment

nodes.label: Same-model echo chamber baseline
nodes.content: When both agents share the same model (gpt-5.2) and differ only in persona, the structural diversity of the KG should converge faster, producing lower CSER than cross-vendor conditions.

nodes.label: Persona diversity compensates model homogeneity
nodes.content: Hypothesis: assigning maximally contrasting personas (critic vs synthesizer) to the same model can partially compensate for vendor homogeneity, yielding CSER > 0.25 (above pure echo chamber).

nodes.label: KG-2 experimental design note
nodes.content: KG-2 is cell (same-model, same-vendor) in the 2x2 vendor-diversity experiment. Comparison targets: KG-main (cross, OpenAI+human), KG-3 (cross-vendor, GPT+Gemini), KG-4 (same-vendor, Gemini+Gemini).

edges.label: Baseline echo chamber hypothesis motivates persona compensation test
Persona hypothesis is tested within 2x2 experimental design
Experimental design grounds the echo chamber baseline
